# Ella's milestones*Created: Saturday, December 22, 2018 at 1:49:54?PM*<div>Ella's milestones</div>
<div><br></div>
<ul>
<li>Mama - Thanksgiving, 7 months</li>
<li>Papa - December 21, 8 months</li>
<li>First Tooth -  December 18, 7 months 5 days</li>
<li>First swing ride and 2.5 nap - December 24, 8 month 1 day</li>
<li>First time in a high chair at A Good Morning Cafe, our favorite breakfast place - 8 months, 4 days, December 28 2018</li>
<li>Lifts arms up up when we come to pick her up. 12/29/18 8 months and 5-days </li>
<li>Scratched her hair on The right hand side because it was itchy while she was being fed by daddy. 12/31/2018 8 months and a week. </li>
<li>Self fed herself her first whole foods cereal organic cheerio after throwing six on the ground - 1/3/19, 8 months and 11 days</li>
</ul>
<div>503-880-1688</div>
<div>9 months! 01/28/2019</div>
<ul>
<li>Three different milestones number one. </li>
<li> She picked up her keyboard with their hands and grab you between her fingers and pulled it up on her own. </li>
<li>Number two. She started counting 123 using bAlls from the play gym. I started teaching her how to count using three different balls go in one, two, three. The way she did this was I would give her one ball she would hold it I would say one she would say make a sound as if she was saying one I would then add a second ball and I would say two and then I will go back and forth 121212 then I would say one again and then she was making noise that sounded like a one then I would handle added second ball and I was it too she made a noise I then added the third ball saying three I also juggling all three ball started with one first and second ball juggling and a dirt ball juggling juggling skills are not that good with three balls but I could juggle two balls pretty well.</li>
<li>Number three the coolest thing IŐve got using the three bars from the play gym throwing them onto the ground spread on the carpet one ball when into a think under her bed the second ball rolled a few feet from her I would say 2 to 3 feet that third ball rolled up behind the door at the entrance door to her room and she was stuck somewhere in the middle she was confused because she didnŐt know which ball to go towards so what she did was instead of rolling to the right towards the ball that was not behind the closed doors she decided to close the door to swing the door back-and-forth in order to see the other ball See the other ball and started swinging the door back-and-forth looking at the ball and thinking that she was going to get that one and after I would say a minute to two minutes of swing of the door back-and-forth because she was amazed by the door swinging back-and-forth she started moving a body towards the ball so she close the door almost fully with their fingers in her hand and went towards the bar that was behind the door and with my enthusiasm and continuous support she pushed yourself towards the ball by swinging swinging and rolling over towards the ball that was by the door.</li>
</ul>
<div>2/1/19</div>
<ul>
<li>Looks like Papa got really excited about the last milestones while Mama was in class!</li>
<li>Ellochka stood on all fours today and didn't fall over! We also signed her up for her first JCC preschool class called yad b yad, which she will attend with me or my mom in the mornings to learn to swim and learn about Shabbat and meet the other children. I'm so excited to have her exposed to this wonderful environment with special director Lana who was my JCC taenna preschool teacher. Can't wait to have her exposed to Jewish values and traditions. I hope that she will enjoy it. She is sleeping and I miss her so much right now. ?</li>
<li>February 15, 9 months and 22 days</li>
<li>Ellochka started crawling today! It's more like swimming slowly on the carpet but she is moving forward and grabbing toys! Can't wait to see what comes next!</li>
<li>February 16, 9 months and 23 days</li>
<li>In addition to her two top teeth,Ellochka now has her lower right front tooth coming out. Very sharp. She bit Laura and our fingers!</li>
<li>3/15 10 months 3 weeks sounded like she said, &quotdad I love you Dad&quot on video cam</li>
<li>3/22 2 days away from 11 months! Looking for new nannies. Ellochka is crawling on knees now. Likes to dip underwater in the tub and scream when unhappy. She knows what she wants. ?? she is getting older and missing us. It makes me miss her more. I wish I had more time with her. </li>
</ul>
<div><br></div>
<div>7/31/19 - 1 year 2 mo</div>
<ul>
<li>Ella walked 7-10 steps with Leon! Then on 8/1 she walked many times before I left to work. Such an incredible sight to see!</li>
</ul>
<div>9/10/19 - 1 year 4 mo </div>
<ul>
<li>Ella said thank you! It's been a while since I logged. She also says na, dai, da, Google, mama, papa, dada, dadday, namnam(hungry), dexik (Dexter), Kika (Kiska), Dede (Google) AND SHE COUNTS IN RUSSIAN (ras, da, ti, cheche, Pa!)</li>
<li>9/24/19 - 1 year 4 almost 1 year 5. </li>
<li>Ella is starting to bring out her consciousness. SheŐs gentle when she sleeps- baby, but when sheÔs cranky oh, boy...watch out. But I love her. ;)</li>
<li>10/9/19 - 17.5 months </li>
<li>She's starting to buckle herself or wants to buckle herself. Tells you to do it if she doesn't get buckled. </li>
<li>Very opinionated already. </li>
<li>Says ŇkiskaÓ very clearly and wants to kiss her</li>
<li>Like to play tag and be chased </li>
<li>Points to objects and says nya nya! We canŐt figure out what she wants!</li>
<li>Nanny quit two days ago. Looking for a new one or day care. Mostly worried Ella will have to readjust and that I wonŐt be able to watch her on camera when we miss her dur</li>
<li>5/14 - so much happened. Where to start. Ella counted to 5 today in English. She also played advanced Japanese and Swiss toys for 4yr olds and had memorized 50% of 500 flash cards her daddy got her. Tfu tfu. Laura calls her a computer. We love spending so much time with her. May we all and our families and loved ones be safe from Coronavirus and all health ailments now and forever. </li>
<li><br></li>
</ul>
<div><br></div>
<div><br></div>
<div>Need to teach Ella conflict and resolution skills. Both are super important to learn and be taught. </div>
<div><br></div>
<ul>
<li>5/12/22 4 yrs- so much has happened since I last logged. Ella is now in a really funny stage where she is her own person. There are too many amazing things to list now but one adorable thing she does is say Ňmmmwah! Eeeya!Ó (Representing a kiss and strong hug) before saying goodbye to us </li>
</ul>
<div>8/22/23</div>
<ul>
<li>Yesterday was the first day of kindergarten. Ella went to Pinewood and did the summer camp for four weeks and my God we donŐt even recognize her. SheŐs so different and growing so much become more confident outspoken calmer. Of course there are the occasional outburst here and there which we are working on but I am so proud of her and she amazes me every day. Today she came home from school. I picked her up and she showed me her materials from her math club and she told me that she came up to a girl name Soraya and asked her to play first which I was so proud about because that means sheŐs so brave and overcame her fear of talking to other kids first, and then when she built some amazing houses out of magna tiles, she stood next to them while being videotaped and said hi, I am Ella and I made this beautiful house and spoke about it so eloquently and I was so proud of her. I canŐt wait to see her learn and blossom and grow and find her passion and IŐm so glad that sheŐs a Pinewood so I can help develop her in the best possible way. I love her so much. She has changed so much. She has such a wonderful, loving attitude and she has the most beautiful blue eyes and long eyelashes and long blonde hair.</li>
<li>January 16 5 3/4 years old Ella had a lot of wonderful breakthroughs today but really the last few months have been magical. She has completely transformed from a child who cannot easily control her upset emotions to a loving, kind calm collected older sister, who sets the best example for her little sister. She has grown so much and has learned so much and has just blossomed up Pinewood. She even recently started reading, which is the most exciting thing ever to her and to us. Today was the first time that she spelled out award and wrote it down. She had done something to upset her sister and then decided to go to her room to write her a card, telling us not to come in and not to look until sheŐs done. When she came out, she showed us the most beautiful card for Leila, which showed a picture of her in the house And then she wrote the letters SOWEE or something like this and it was the first time that she spelled A word she spelled sorry another thing that she did was she got undressed by herself, got into the bathtub without being asked then she, when I was feeding them dinner while Yonah was on a phone call she asked me for a fork, and then said, never mind mom IŐll get it myself, and then when I had Finished eating I pretended like I could not. My eyes were not working and I said my eyes must not be working because thereŐs a empty plates on the table and I know that my children would never leave their empty plates on the table and Ella told me to close my eyes, and after five minutes I was only allowed to open them and not only did she clean her plate and LeilaÔs plate and all of the food off the table And all of the utensils but she also wiped down the table with a wipe. So this day I had to record it, even though Ella has been surprising us daily, because itŐs a perfect embodiment of the girl she is growing into, and it is beyond a relief and makes me and Yana so happy every day. We are so so proud of her and only wants the very very best for her and of course for Leila too. We love how close the girls are and how they are so bonded together. Everybody always tells us that it is very special how close they are. Of course they have their little arguments and bicker but at the end of the day they love each other and they will do anything for each other and that is what we wish for them always.</li>
</ul>
<div><br></div>
<div>4/9/24 almost 6!</div>
<div>EllaŐs current strengths - there are more!!</div>
<div><b>Great sense of humor, makes me laugh, excellent problem solver.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Innovative with solutions to everyday problems.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Excellent performer, talented on stage, loves to sing and memorize lines.</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Confident in performing in front of classmates or family members.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Assertive when facing unfair treatment towards herself or others.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Strong moral compass, respects correctness and rules.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Increasingly outgoing.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Musically talented, adapts to different styles, sings wonderful melodies.</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Curious about science and math, enjoys experimenting and understanding how things work.</b></div>
<div><b>Ą Caring and protective towards her sister.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Determined, persistent in the face of challenges, does not give up easily.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Loyal friend, inclusive, ensures no one feels left out.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Quick learner with a great memory, often remembers things that even I canŐt.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Independent with strong leadership qualities, confident in directing others.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Made breakfast last Saturday as a surprise, showing initiative.</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Creative in drawing and art, finds passion and relief in long periods of concentration.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Empathetic, capable of understanding othersŐ perspectives.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Considerate of othersŐ feelings, ensures they are not hurt.Ę</b></div>
<div><b>ĘĄ Skilled dancer.</b></div>
